tasks.title: CharacterController with third-person movement
tasks.description: Implement InsimulCharacterController.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/CharacterController.ts. Use Unity's CharacterController component for third-person movement with WASD/gamepad input, sprint (Left Shift), jump (Space), and collision. Load the player character model from bundled Quaternius assets (Vincent-frontFacing or selected avatar). Use the new Input System for input mapping. Handle ground detection via CharacterController.isGrounded, slope limits, and step offset. Integrate with CameraManager for camera-relative movement direction.

tasks.title: CameraManager with orbit and follow modes
tasks.description: Implement CameraManager.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/CameraManager.ts. Use Cinemachine FreeLook camera for orbit around player with mouse/right-stick rotation, zoom with scroll wheel, and obstacle avoidance via CinemachineCollider. Handle smooth transitions between exterior camera (far orbit), interior camera (closer/lower orbit), and dialogue camera (focus on NPC face) using Cinemachine blending. Camera must avoid clipping through walls and terrain.

tasks.title: NPCModularAssembler with Quaternius character parts
tasks.description: Implement NPCModularAssembler.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCModularAssembler.ts and QuaterniusNPCLoader.ts. Assemble NPC characters from modular Quaternius parts loaded via Resources.Load: base body (5+ types by gender/build), hair (100+ styles with gender affinity), outfits (200+ pieces organized by gender/set). Use NPCModelManifest.ts selection logic: genre (fantasy/scifi/modern/generic) + gender + body type (average/athletic/heavy/slim) + role (guard/merchant/civilian). Attach parts to SkinnedMeshRenderer bone hierarchy. Fallback chain: role-specific -> body type -> genre+gender -> generic.

tasks.title: NPCAccessorySystem for visual variety
tasks.description: Implement NPCAccessorySystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCAccessorySystem.ts and NPCAppearanceGenerator.ts. Attach accessories (hats, glasses, jewelry, weapons, tools) to NPC skeleton at appropriate bone transforms. Generate appearance variations deterministically from character seed so each NPC looks unique. Accessories should match the NPC's occupation and social status. Use the prop models from bundled assets.

tasks.title: NPCAnimationController with Animator state machine
tasks.description: Implement NPCAnimationController.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCAnimationController.ts and AnimationAssetManager.ts. Create an Animator Controller with states: idle, walk, run, talk, sit, work, eat, sleep, interact, combat. Use blend trees for locomotion (idle->walk->run based on speed parameter). Load animation clips from bundled collection. Support occupation-specific work animations (blacksmith hammering, merchant gesturing, guard patrolling). Use Animator.CrossFade for smooth transitions.

tasks.title: NPCMovementController with NavMesh pathfinding
tasks.description: Implement NPCMovementController.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCMovementController.ts, PathfindingSystem.ts, and NavigationSystem.ts. Use Unity NavMeshAgent for NPC navigation. Bake NavMesh at runtime after terrain and buildings are generated. Support patrol routes, wandering within radius, walking to specific buildings, and following roads. NavMeshAgent obstacle avoidance handles NPC-NPC and NPC-player collisions. Movement speed varies by activity (strolling vs rushing to work). Integrate with NPCLocationCycler.ts for cycling between assigned locations.

tasks.title: NPCScheduleSystem with daily routines
tasks.description: Implement NPCScheduleSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCScheduleSystem.ts, ScheduleExecutor.ts, and OccupationActivitySystem.ts. Each NPC follows a daily schedule from NPCIR.schedule (wake/sleep times, work hours, eating, socializing). NPCs use NavMeshAgent to move between home, workplace, tavern, town square based on time of day. Sync with DayNightCycle. NPCs should visibly perform activities at each location — play sit animation at tables, work animation at stations, sleep animation in beds.

tasks.title: NPCSimulationLOD for performance scaling
tasks.description: Implement NPCSimulationLOD.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCSimulationLOD.ts. Scale NPC simulation detail based on distance from player: nearby NPCs get full animation/pathfinding/interaction, mid-range NPCs get simplified movement (less frequent NavMesh updates), distant NPCs become billboard impostors or are deactivated. Limit to MAX_NPCS (8) fully-simulated NPCs at once. Use LODGroup and coroutine-based update throttling for performance.

tasks.title: NPCGreetingSystem and interaction prompts
tasks.description: Implement NPCGreetingSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCGreetingSystem.ts, NPCInteractionPrompt.ts, and InteractionPromptSystem.ts. When the player approaches an NPC within range, show a Canvas world-space interaction prompt (E to talk). NPCs greet the player with contextual lines based on relationship, time of day, and current activity. Display NPC name and occupation as a world-space TextMeshPro label above their head. Use NPCTalkingIndicator.ts for speech bubble sprite when NPC is speaking.

tasks.title: NPCActivityLabelSystem and talking indicators
tasks.description: Implement NPCActivityLabelSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCActivityLabelSystem.ts and NPCTalkingIndicator.ts. Show world-space Canvas labels above NPCs indicating their current activity (Working, Eating, Socializing, Resting, Shopping). Display speech bubble indicator when NPC is in conversation. Labels should use LookAt camera (billboard) and alpha-fade with distance. Use TextMeshPro for crisp text rendering.

tasks.title: AmbientConversationSystem for NPC-NPC chat
tasks.description: Implement AmbientConversationSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/AmbientConversationSystem.ts and NPCAmbientConversationManager.ts. When two NPCs are near each other (e.g., at tavern, town square), they should engage in ambient conversations with world-space speech bubble UI. Use NPCSocializationController.ts logic for choosing conversation partners based on relationships and personality compatibility. Conversations affect NPC relationships via RelationshipManager.

tasks.title: Chat panel — dialogue UI with LLM integration
tasks.description: Implement dialogue UI Canvas panel matching shared/game-engine/rendering/BabylonChatPanel.ts. Full chat panel with: TMP_InputField for player messages, ScrollRect conversation history with chat bubbles, NPC portrait/name display, typing indicator animation while waiting for LLM response. Support BabylonDialogueActions.ts action buttons (Ask about quest, Trade, Request help, Say goodbye) as Button components. Panel slides in with DOTween or Unity animation when conversation starts. Must handle multi-turn conversations with full history.

tasks.title: LLM dialogue integration via AI service
tasks.description: Implement InsimulAIService.cs matching the in-app game's LLM conversation system (shared/game-engine/logic/LocalNpcConversation.ts). Send player messages to configured AI endpoint (Gemini API, local Ollama, or custom) via UnityWebRequest. Include NPC system prompt from dialogueContexts in SystemsIR (personality, occupation, knowledge, relationship with player, current truths). Use coroutines for streaming responses with real-time text display. Support conversation memory across multiple interactions with the same NPC. Handle API key configuration in PlayerPrefs or settings JSON.

tasks.title: LipSyncController for NPC speech
tasks.description: Implement LipSyncController.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/LipSyncController.ts. Animate NPC face BlendShape weights or bone-based jaw movement in sync with dialogue text display speed. Support StreamingAudioPlayer.ts integration for TTS AudioClip playback if available. Even without TTS, NPCs should have mouth movement animation timed to their dialogue text scrolling. Use SkinnedMeshRenderer.SetBlendShapeWeight for viseme-based lip shapes.

tasks.title: NPCBusinessInteractionSystem for shops and services
tasks.description: Implement NPCBusinessInteractionSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/NPCBusinessInteractionSystem.ts and BabylonShopPanel.ts. When interacting with merchant NPCs, open a shop Canvas UI showing their inventory with buy/sell prices in a grid layout. Support business types from BusinessIR: blacksmith, tavern, market, general store, etc. Each business has appropriate items based on type. Include a notice board Canvas panel (BabylonNoticeBoardPanel.ts equivalent) for settlement notice boards with quests and announcements.

tasks.title: GameMenuSystem with all panels
tasks.description: Implement full game menu Canvas UI matching shared/game-engine/rendering/GameMenuSystem.ts. Pause menu with tab buttons for: Resume, Inventory, Quest Journal, Map, Skills, Rules, Settings, Save/Load, Quit. ESC key toggles menu and sets Time.timeScale=0. Each tab activates the corresponding panel GameObject. Match the UIIR configuration from the IR for which panels are enabled. Include GenreUIManager.ts logic to show/hide genre-specific panels (e.g., language panels only for language-learning genre).

tasks.title: Inventory system with equipment
tasks.description: Implement inventory Canvas UI matching shared/game-engine/rendering/BabylonInventory.ts and logic/EquipmentManager.ts. Grid-based inventory using Unity UI GridLayoutGroup with item icon Images, stack count text, tooltip on hover showing item description. Support item categories: weapons, armor, consumables, quest items, books, crafting materials using tabs. Drag-and-drop (IBeginDragHandler/IDropHandler) for equipping. Equipment slots on character paper doll panel. Use ItemThumbnailRenderer.ts approach: render 3D item model to RenderTexture for preview icons.

tasks.title: Quest tracking and journal UI
tasks.description: Implement quest UI matching shared/game-engine/rendering/BabylonQuestTracker.ts and QuestOfferPanel.ts. HUD quest tracker: always-visible panel listing active quest objectives with checkbox progress. Full quest journal panel: scrollable list of all quests (active/completed/failed) with descriptions and objective breakdowns. Quest offer popup when NPC offers a quest with Accept/Decline buttons. Integrate with QuestIndicatorManager.ts for floating quest markers (!) as world-space sprites above NPC heads and QuestWaypointManager.ts for navigation waypoint arrows.

tasks.title: QuestCompletionManager and notification system
tasks.description: Implement QuestCompletionManager.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/QuestCompletionManager.ts, QuestNotificationManager.ts, and QuestNotificationSystem.ts. Detect quest objective completion, trigger completion events via C# events/delegates, award rewards (XP, items, reputation). Show notification toasts that slide in from screen edge for quest updates (new quest, objective complete, quest complete). Support QuestHotspotManager.ts for trigger-collider-based quest triggers. Integrate with logic/QuestCompletionEngine.ts and QuestAutoCompletionDetector.ts.

tasks.title: CombatSystem with multiple combat modes
tasks.description: Implement combat systems matching shared/game-engine/rendering/CombatSystem.ts, TurnBasedCombatSystem.ts, FightingCombatSystem.ts, and RangedCombatSystem.ts. Support the genre-appropriate combat mode from CombatIR: real-time action combat (hitbox/hurtbox with Collider triggers), turn-based RPG combat (sequential action selection UI), or ranged combat (projectile physics with Rigidbody). Include CombatUI.ts HUD elements (Slider health bars, action buttons, crosshair Image). HealthBar.ts as world-space Canvas for enemy health. Combat uses stats from CharacterIR.

tasks.title: ResourceSystem with gathering mechanics
tasks.description: Implement ResourceSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/ResourceSystem.ts and gathering sub-systems: logic/FishingSystem.ts, logic/MiningSystem.ts, logic/HerbalismSystem.ts. Player can gather resources from world objects (mine ore nodes, fish in water, pick herbs) via interaction triggers. Each system has its own mini-game UI panel or interaction pattern (fishing: timing bar, mining: hold interaction, herbalism: proximity collection). Resources add to inventory and feed into crafting. Only enabled when ResourcesIR is present.

tasks.title: CraftingSystem and recipe UI
tasks.description: Implement CraftingSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/logic/CraftingSystem.ts and logic/RecipeCraftingSystem.ts. Recipe-based crafting using gathered resources and purchased materials. Crafting Canvas UI: left panel lists available recipes (filtered by owned materials), right panel shows required ingredients with have/need counts and result preview. Support crafting stations at appropriate businesses (forge at blacksmith, kitchen at tavern, workbench at lumbermill) via trigger collider proximity. Crafted items add to inventory.

tasks.title: ExplorationDiscoverySystem with rewards
tasks.description: Implement ExplorationDiscoverySystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/ExplorationDiscoverySystem.ts. Track which areas the player has visited using trigger zones per settlement/landmark. Award discovery bonuses (XP, items) for finding new settlements, landmarks, hidden areas. Show discovery notification toast. Integrate with logic/KnowledgeCollectionSystem.ts for collecting world lore entries into a codex. Support ContextualHintSystem.ts for floating hint text guiding players toward undiscovered content.

tasks.title: ReputationManager and relationship tracking
tasks.description: Implement ReputationManager.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/ReputationManager.ts, logic/RelationshipManager.ts, and logic/RomanceSystem.ts. Track player reputation per settlement and faction as float values. Track individual NPC relationships (friendship, trust, romance) with thresholds for relationship level changes. Reputation affects shop prices (multiplier), quest availability (gate by minimum rep), NPC dialogue tone (passed to AI service context). Relationship changes based on conversation choices, quest completion, and player actions.

tasks.title: AnimalNPCSystem with ambient wildlife
tasks.description: Implement AnimalNPCSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/AnimalNPCSystem.ts and logic/AmbientLifeBehaviorSystem.ts. Spawn animal GameObjects from IR animals data (cats, dogs, birds, horses, etc.) using bundled animal GLTF models via Resources.Load. Animals use simplified NavMeshAgent for wandering. Wild animals flee from player (increase agent speed away), pets follow (set agent destination to player). Support VehicleSystem.ts for rideable animals (horses, alpacas) with mount/dismount interaction. Animals add ambient life to settlements.

tasks.title: AudioManager with spatial and ambient audio
tasks.description: Implement AudioManager.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/AudioManager.ts, EnvironmentalAudioManager.ts, and logic/AmbientSoundSystem.ts. Play ambient AudioSource (looping) per worldType from audio manifest. Spatial AudioSource on NPCs for conversations, animals for sounds, water for ambient loops. Footstep AudioSource on player that changes AudioClip based on surface via Raycast material detection (stone, grass, wood, metal). Door sound on building entry/exit. Support StreamingAudioPlayer.ts for TTS AudioClip streaming via UnityWebRequest.

tasks.title: PhotographySystem — screenshot capture
tasks.description: Implement PhotographySystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/BabylonPhotographySystem.ts and BabylonPhotoBookPanel.ts. Photo mode: set Time.timeScale=0, switch to free camera (Cinemachine override), apply post-processing filters (vignette, color grading), capture via ScreenCapture.CaptureScreenshotAsTexture(). Photo book Canvas panel to review captured screenshots in a grid. Save photos to Application.persistentDataPath. Key exploration feature.

tasks.title: PuzzleSystem and dungeon generation
tasks.description: Implement PuzzleSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/PuzzleSystem.ts, BabylonPuzzleUI.ts, and ProceduralDungeonGenerator.ts. Generate dungeons from IR dungeon data: create room GameObjects connected by corridor meshes, spawn enemy NPCs and loot containers per room spec. Puzzles appear as interactable objects with their own Canvas overlay UI for puzzle interaction. Support multiple puzzle types (pattern matching, sequence, etc.). Dungeon generation uses seeded PRNG for deterministic layouts.

tasks.title: GameIntroSequence and narrative cutscenes
tasks.description: Implement GameIntroSequence.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/GameIntroSequence.ts and NarrativeCutscenePanel.ts. Play intro sequence when starting a new game: fullscreen Canvas overlay with NarrativeIR text, character portrait Images, background art Image, and optional AudioSource narration. Support mid-game narrative cutscenes triggered by quest milestones via C# event subscription. Use DOTween or Animator for text fade/slide transitions. Include MainMenuScreen.ts equivalent: main menu scene with New Game, Continue, Settings, Quit buttons.

tasks.title: OnboardingManager and tutorial system
tasks.description: Implement OnboardingManager.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/OnboardingLauncher.ts and logic/OnboardingManager.ts. Step-by-step tutorial guiding the player through basic controls (movement, camera, interaction, inventory, dialogue, combat). Highlight UI elements with glow Image overlay and arrow pointer. Tutorial steps from shared/onboarding/ definitions. Support logic/TutorialQuestSystem.ts for tutorial quests that teach game mechanics through guided objectives.

tasks.title: ActionQuickBar and player action system
tasks.description: Implement ActionQuickBar.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/ActionQuickBar.ts, BabylonRadialMenu.ts, and logic/PlayerActionTracker.ts. HUD hotbar: horizontal Canvas panel with 9 assignable action slots bound to 1-9 keys, showing item/ability icons. Radial menu: hold middle mouse to show circular Canvas menu for quick access to common actions. Track player actions for quest completion detection. Support PlayerActionSystem.ts for executing actions (use item, cast spell, interact). Include logic/WorldObjectActionManager.ts for context-sensitive object interactions.

tasks.title: RuleEnforcer and truth sync
tasks.description: Implement RuleEnforcer.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/RuleEnforcer.ts and logic/GameTruthSync.ts. Evaluate world rules from SystemsIR.rules against current game state using a condition evaluator. Rules gate content, trigger events, and modify NPC behavior. Sync truths (world facts stored as Dictionary<string,object>) as they change from player actions and quest progress. Support logic/ContentGatingManager.ts for locking/unlocking content based on rule evaluation. Include logic/TemporaryStateSystem.ts for time-limited status effects that auto-expire.

tasks.title: Save/Load system with multiple playthroughs
tasks.description: Implement SaveSystem.cs matching shared/game-engine/rendering/SaveIndicator.ts and logic/RunManager.ts. Auto-save at configurable intervals with visual indicator (spinning icon). Manual save/load from game menu with slot selection. Serialize game state to JSON: player position, inventory, quest progress, NPC relationships, reputation, discovered areas, game time. Save to Application.persistentDataPath. Support logic/SaveConflictResolver.ts for handling save conflicts. PlaythroughMainMenu.ts for managing multiple save slots with world preview.

tasks.title: SkillTreePanel and document reading
tasks.description: Implement skill tree and document Canvas panels matching shared/game-engine/rendering/BabylonSkillTreePanel.ts, DocumentReadingPanel.ts, and BabylonRulesPanel.ts. Skill tree panel: node graph UI with skill icons connected by lines, click to unlock with skill points. Document reading panel: opens when player interacts with books/scrolls, showing TextIR content with TextMeshPro pagination (previous/next page buttons). Rules panel: scrollable list of active world rules that affect gameplay. Support CompositionWritingUI.ts for player text composition with TMP_InputField.

tasks.title: VR support scaffolding
tasks.description: Implement VR scaffolding matching the in-app game's VR systems: VRManager.ts, VRHandTrackingManager.ts, VRInteractionManager.ts, VRHUDManager.ts, VRChatPanel.ts, VRCombatAdapter.ts, VRUIPanel.ts, VRVocabularyLabels.ts, VRAccessibilityManager.ts, and logic/VRComfortSettings.ts. Use Unity XR Interaction Toolkit with OpenXR. Create XR Rig with hand tracking (XRDirectInteractor), teleport/smooth locomotion (XRRayInteractor + TeleportationProvider), world-space Canvas UI panels for VR, and VR interaction system (grab, poke). Accessibility: vignette tunneling, snap turn, seated mode.
